2605.22379.title: Cross-Subject EEG Emotion Recognition Based on Temporal Asynchronous Alignment Contrastive Learning
2605.22379.paper_summary: With the advancement of science and technology, the importance of emotion research has become increasingly evident. Electroencephalography (EEG)-based emotion recognition has emerged as an active research area in recent years, owing to its objectivity and high temporal resolution. However, most existing methods focus on optimizing encoder structures to enhance feature extraction capabilities, while paying relatively little attention to similarity calculation strategies, particularly overlooking the potential temporal misalignment of responses among different subjects. To address these shortcomings, this paper draws inspiration from the late interaction mechanism of ColBERT in natural language processing (NLP) and proposes a Temporal Asynchronous Alignment-based Contrastive Learning (TA2CL) framework. This method transforms the traditional global "hard alignment" similarity calculation approach into a fine-grained local matching mechanism, enabling the model to adaptively search for and align "locally highly correlated" segments between two EEG signals, thereby effectively mitigating the effects of inter-subject differences and temporal delays. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves strong performance across multiple public datasets. Specifically, on the FACED dataset, it achieves an accuracy of 64.5% for the nine-class classification task and 79.5% for the binary classification task, while on the SEED and SEED-V datasets, it achieves accuracies of 86.4% and 70.1%, respectively, validating the method's effectiveness and generalization capability.
2605.22379.paper_summary_zh: 隨著科學和技術的進步，情感研究的重要性變得越來越明顯。基於腦電圖（EEG）的情感識別在近年來成為一個活躍的研究領域，這得益於其客觀性和高時間解析度。然而，大多數現有方法專注於優化編碼器結構以增強特徵提取能力，而相對較少關注相似性計算策略，特別是忽視了不同受試者之間反應的潛在時間錯位。為了解決這些不足，本文受到了自然語言處理（NLP）中ColBERT的晚期互動機制的啟發，提出了一種基於時間異步對齊的對比學習（TA2CL）框架。這種方法將傳統的全局“硬對齊”相似性計算方法轉變為一種細粒度的局部匹配機制，使模型能夠自適應地搜尋並對齊兩個EEG信號之間“局部高度相關”的片段，從而有效減輕受試者之間差異和時間延遲的影響。實驗結果表明，所提出的方法在多個公共數據集上達到了良好的表現。具體而言，在FACED數據集上，該方法在九類分類任務中達到了64.5%的準確率，在二元分類任務中達到了79.5%的準確率，而在SEED和SEED-V數據集上，分別達到了86.4%和70.1%的準確率，驗證了該方法的有效性和泛化能力。
2605.22379.author: Ying Xie et.al.
2605.22379.authors: Ying Xie, Yi Zheng, Zehui Xiao, Wenkai Lu, Mengting Liu
